intro: The Department of Citywide Administrative Services (DCAS) was created in 1996 when Mayor Rudolph Giuliani merged the Department of General Services and the Department of Personnel into a single agency. DCAS now handles the internal machinery of city government: recruiting, hiring, and training municipal employees; managing 55 public buildings; buying, selling, and leasing city property; and purchasing over $1 billion a year in goods and services for other agencies.
